key_insight: PRIME restores learning capacity in non-stationary multi-agent UAV communication by reinitializing only neurons that are both activation-dormant and gradient-silent.
engineering_relevance: This helps keep shared-policy multi-agent controllers adaptable during shifting emergency-network conditions without disrupting still-useful representations.
method: Extends the bidirectional Silent Neuron idea to cooperative MARL.
Aggregates activation and gradient statistics across the full team batch.
Resets only neurons that are both dormant and receive no meaningful training gradient.
evidence_signal: On a phase-switching UAV emergency communication simulator, PRIME improved interquartile mean return by 24.9% over MAPPO and reduced dormant neuron fractions to 10–20% from 40–45%.
watch_item: Because the method depends on team-level activation and gradient statistics, implementation quality and threshold choices may affect when neurons are reset.
paper_title: PRIME: Plasticity Recovery in Multi-Agent Environments for UAV-Assisted Emergency Communication Networks
